name: Motril – circular route through the city
name_original: Motril – ruta circular por la ciudad
region_intro: Hiking around Motril presents a varied landscape, combining the coastal beauty of the Costa Tropical with the mountainous terrain of the Sierra Nevada foothills. The region features dramatic cliffs, agricultural areas with tropical plantations, and accessible trails through natural parks. This unique geographical position allows for routes ranging from sea-level strolls to higher elevation paths offering panoramic views. Best hiking trails around Motril The most popular hiking route is Motril – circular route through the city, a 2.5 miles (4.1 km) trail that takes 1 hour 5 minutes to complete. This easy route offers an accessible way to explore the city's surroundings. Another top favourite among local hikers is Cliffs of Playa de la Joya – Jolúcar Glass Walkway loop from Torrenueva Costa, a moderate 6.7 miles (10.8 km) path. This route features coastal views and dramatic cliff formations. Local hikers also love the Poniente Beach, Motril – Playa del Poniente de Motril loop from Reserva Natural Concertada Charca Suárez, a 3.8 miles (6.1 km) trail leading through coastal areas, often completed in about 1 hour 33 minutes. Hiking around Motril is defined by coastal paths, agricultural landscapes, and mountain trails. The network offers options for various ability levels, from easy coastal walks to more challenging routes in the foothills. The routes in Motril are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 200 reviews. More than 1000 hikers have used komoot to explore Motril's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Motril?
A: Motril offers a diverse selection of over 60 hiking trails. These routes cater to various preferences, ranging from easy coastal walks to more challenging mountain paths in the foothills of the Sierra Nevada.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see on hikes near Motril?
A: Hiking around Motril provides a unique blend of coastal and mountainous scenery. You can explore dramatic cliffs along the Mediterranean, tranquil agricultural areas with tropical plantations like avocado and sugarcane, and ascend into the foothills of the Sierra Nevada National Park for breathtaking panoramic views. Areas like Parque Natural de las Haciendas offer a mix of mountains and oak forests, while the Guadalfeo River Trail winds through rural landscapes.

Q: Are there easy hiking options suitable for beginners or families in Motril?
A: Yes, Motril has plenty of easy hiking options perfect for beginners or families. Approximately half of the trails are rated as easy. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e985085704/motril-circular-route-through-the-city" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Motril – circular route through the city&lt;/a&gt; is an accessible 4.1 km path. The Parque de los Pueblos de América also offers tranquil, easily accessible paths suitable for all skill levels amidst exotic plants.

Q: Are there any challenging hikes for experienced trekkers in the Motril area?
A: For experienced trekkers, Motril offers more demanding routes, particularly in the higher elevations. The trail to the summit of Sierra de Lújar provides spectacular views and more challenging terrain. Additionally, the Sierra Nevada National Park, a short drive from Motril, features a vast network of trails with significant elevation changes and rugged landscapes.

Q: What are some interesting landmarks or natural attractions to see along the hiking trails?
A: Many trails offer access to fascinating natural and historical sites. You can explore coastal formations like the Cliffs of Playa de la Joya, or discover highlights such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4692443" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Charca Suárez&lt;/a&gt;, a lake, or the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3532436" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rules Reservoir dam&lt;/a&gt;. The region also has several caves, including the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5531922" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Captain's Cave Tunnel&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5538122" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Olías Caves&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there circular hiking routes available in Motril?
A: Yes, many of the hiking routes around Motril are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14599447" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cliffs of Playa de la Joya – Jolúcar Glass Walkway loop from Torrenueva Costa&lt;/a&gt;, which offers stunning coastal views.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Motril?
A: The best time for hiking in Motril is generally from May to October. During these months, the mild temperatures are ideal for exploring both coastal and mountain trails. While higher altitudes in the Sierra Nevada offer snow in colder months, the spring and autumn provide pleasant conditions across the region, avoiding the intense summer heat.

Q: Are dogs allowed on hiking trails around Motril?
A: Many natural areas and trails in Motril are dog-friendly, but it's always recommended to check specific regulations for each route or protected area. In general, dogs are welcome on a leash, especially on less crowded paths. Always ensure you clean up after your pet.

Q: Is public transport available to reach hiking trailheads in Motril?
A: While some trailheads within Motril's urban or coastal areas might be accessible by local bus services, many of the more remote or mountainous trails, especially those leading into the Sierra Nevada foothills, are best reached by car. It's advisable to check local bus schedules and routes for specific starting points.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy the most about hiking in Motril?
A: The hiking routes in Motril are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 200 reviews. Hikers often praise the region's diverse scenery, from stunning coastal views to the unique blend of agricultural landscapes and mountain vistas. The variety of trails catering to different fitness levels is also frequently highlighted.

Q: Are there any permits required for hiking in the Motril area?
A: For most general hiking trails around Motril, including coastal paths and local parks, no specific permits are required. However, if you plan to venture into certain protected areas within the Sierra Nevada National Park, especially for specific activities or in sensitive zones, it's always wise to check with the park authorities for any necessary permits or regulations.

Q: Where can I find parking for hiking trails in Motril?
A: Parking availability varies by trailhead. For urban and coastal routes, you can often find street parking or designated public parking areas nearby. For trails leading into natural parks or more remote areas, there are usually small parking lots or roadside spaces at common starting points. It's recommended to plan your route and check for parking options in advance.
tips: Building built in 1955, with a simple and functional architecture. Inside there are two floors, with a central staircase that serves as the axis for the two patio bodies that separate specific uses (fishmonger, greengrocer...)

Built in 1880 by some representatives of the local bourgeoisie, the Calderón theater more than responded to the representation needs of this social class, in full swing in the last third of the 19th century, thanks to the benefits generated by the revitalization of sugarcane agriculture. . This theater is conceived as an "Italian theater" with Neoclassical decoration. On the outside, it was not conceived as an emblematic building for the city, but was given the distinct character of a private civil building perfectly integrated into the square and the surrounding historic buildings. The exterior sobriety contrasts with the interior ornamental richness.

The current town hall building dates from 1631 and was built on the ruins of a previous one destroyed after the explosion of a powder keg due to a Berber attack in 1620. It is the work of the master builder from Motril, Isidro de la Chica, and the original work is preserved. Today the main façade and corridor follow the scheme of a stately home of the time.

It was built between 1510 and 1514 on the site of the old Alixara mosque, in an area further north than what had been the center of the Nasrid town. Its construction was the work of the master builder from Granada, Alonso Márquez, who built a sober Gothic-Mudejar temple with a rectangular nave fitted with large arches, with four pairs of side chapels between buttresses, and a slightly wider main chapel. The latter would disappear at the beginning of the 17th century when the extension of the temple was projected in 1603 by adding a transept of a markedly classicist nature.

Its construction dates back to 1538. It used to be the hermitage of San Roque, built to commemorate the victims of the plague epidemic, and the cross in front of the façade reminds us of this.
In the 18th century, its dedication was changed to the Virgen del Carmen. It is proto-baroque in style and consists of a single nave, a hemispherical dome over the main chapel and a dressing room highly decorated with baroque plasterwork.

The Parque de los Pueblos de América is located south of the urban center of Motril. It is one of the main green lungs of the city. This park was born as a link with the American continent, this being reflected through its flora. When walking through the park, among its paths we can appreciate a great variety of species of trees, shrubs and palm trees, all of them from different parts of America.

It was originally built on the citadel that defended Motril in Muslim times, the residence of Queen Aixa Alhorra, mother of Boabdil. The construction of the temple began in 1631, by Isidro de la Chica, in a very austere proto-baroque style.
The building, with a Latin cross, consists of a single nave, a transept covered with a hemispherical dome, a raised main chapel and a dressing room highly decorated with baroque plasterwork.
